body_txt: In reality , some girls like ponies. In TV Land, all girls ( tomboy and girly girl alike) like ponies. Especially the Spoiled Brats . This especially applies to girls who are young enough to be in the Princess Phase . "I want a pony" tends to be the stock Christmas/birthday wish that has no chance of ever coming true. If it's a plot point that a girl used to own a pony, this marks her as a Fallen Princess or possibly an Upper-Class Equestrian . Of course, you don't have to be a girl to like ponies , and creators enjoy the chance to slip in My Little Phony jokes. Even a work that features a Cool Horse isn't immune to this, because who doesn't want a Cool Horse? This trope can overlap with Tomboyish Ponytail , both for the pun and the fact that you want your hair out of your face if you're working or riding, especially outdoors. This is a relatively-recent trope, born only after planes, trains, and automobiles made horses obsolete for day-to-day transportation. Before the 20th century, and in many parts of the world today where horses are still regularly used, there was nothing particularly feminine about liking horses or even ponies; Genghis Khan rode a pony. In fact, horses were more associated with manliness (just ask the Western genre). In most of the modern world, however, horseback riding is more of a hobby (and an expensive one ) than a daily necessity, and female equestrians vastly outnumber the males. Compare Pony Tale (a specific kind of book where the girl-and-pony narrative is central to the plot), Girls Love Stuffed Animals , Pink Means Feminine , Men Like Dogs, Women Like Cats . The Distaff Counterpart to Boys Like Creepy Critters . And expect some Rainbows and Unicorns to show up. No relation to girls driving or liking cool pony cars . That would be this trope.
